lenses.guidance: vv 5-7's exemption list — newly built house, newly planted vineyard, newly betrothed wife — releases men whose lives are mid-completion. The structural narrative privileges full-life over headcount; conquest serves life, not the reverse.

vv 10-18 distinguish distant cities (terms-of-peace first) from Canaanite cities (devoted to destruction). The covenant logic of the redemption arc: peace offered, idolatry rooted out where it would corrupt the promise-land.

vv 1-4's priestly address before battle — 'do not be afraid; do not panic... the LORD your God is with you' — disciplines the heart against numerical anxiety. To trust today is to keep counting Egypt-deliverance higher than enemy chariots.
